summary: Install-time environment exfiltration to third-party telemetry endpoint
details: On npm install, package.json declares a preinstall hook (`node scripts/postinstall.js`) that requires `@doaction/shared/bin/postinstall.js`. The package's own README and src/index.js document the purpose as collecting environment variables via `collectEnv` and shipping them to Datadog through `reportEnvToDatadog` / `sendToDatadog`. The result is unsolicited outbound transmission of the installer's environment to a remote third-party intake endpoint as a side effect of installation, with no opt-in. The package version `9.9.9`, the description "Map-based store with Datadog environment telemetry for internal testing", and the pin on `@doaction/shared@^9.9.9` match the shape of a dependency-confusion artifact: any organization that uses a private `@doaction` scope and resolves the public registry could pull this package and trigger the env beacon. Because the actual sender lives in the unbundled dependency, the exact set of variables transmitted cannot be bounded from this tarball alone, and environment variables on developer/CI machines routinely include credentials (CI tokens, cloud keys, npm tokens).

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
